_meta.status: live as of Phase C2 of the 2.0 federation work package.
_meta.stability: Frozen for the loom-generation lifetime per ADR-002. Shape additions that preserve wire compatibility (new optional response fields) are allowed; breaking changes require a new generation.
_meta.relation_to_classic: Same request shape (issue_ids: list[str]). Response shape differs: classic dashboard returns {updated: list[IssueDict], errors: list[BatchFailure]}; loom returns BatchResponse[SlimIssueLoom] — succeeded: list[SlimIssueLoom] (slim 5-key projection with issue_id), failed: list[BatchFailure]. Loom is the surface that aligns with MCP's container keys (succeeded/failed); classic's {updated, errors} stays frozen per ADR-002.
_meta.fixture_scope: Error paths + all-missing-id 200 (failed[0] pins BatchFailure shape). Populated-success shape (succeeded[0] = SlimIssueLoom) is pinned by the seeded test method TestLoomGenerationParityBatchUpdate::test_succeeded_populated_shape — that requires a real issue id, which the fixture replay cannot provide.

shape_decl.rationale: BatchResponse[_T] (types/api.py) specialised to SlimIssueLoom. Container keys succeeded/failed match MCP batch_update; classic dashboard's {updated, errors} stays frozen for backwards compatibility. Closes the per-generation half of the dashboard-vs-MCP container-key divergence (test_container_key_parity).

examples.name: error_issue_ids_not_list
examples.note: issue_ids must be a JSON array — same validation as classic.

body.error: issue_ids must be a JSON array

examples.name: error_issue_ids_missing
examples.note: Missing issue_ids field is the same shape as the not-a-list case.

body.error: issue_ids must be a JSON array

examples.name: success_all_missing
examples.note: Two non-existent issue_ids: succeeded is empty, failed has one BatchFailure per id with code NOT_FOUND. Pins BatchFailure shape; SlimIssueLoom shape is pinned by the seeded test method.
